criteria.description: deepgram_request_audit.py exists in the repo root and is import-safe: importing the module executes no network, no filesystem reads/writes, and no CLI parsing. All such side effects live under `if __name__ == "__main__":` or inside a main() function guarded that way.
criteria.verifyBy: A test does `import deepgram_request_audit` successfully with no network access and no ~/.voice-tutor-secrets.env present; the graded signal is this behavioral import-safety test passing under `python3 -m unittest discover -v` fully offline.

criteria.description: A pure function parses a Deepgram UTC `created` timestamp tolerantly on Python 3.9: it accepts a trailing 'Z' and returns a timezone-aware UTC datetime equal to the correct instant.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test parses '2024-01-15T08:30:00Z' and asserts the result equals datetime(2024,1,15,8,30,0,tzinfo=timezone.utc).

criteria.description: The same tolerant parser handles the variants Deepgram may send — (a) fractional seconds with a trailing 'Z', (b) a plain UTC timestamp without any 'Z', and (c) fractional seconds without any 'Z' — returning the correct timezone-aware UTC datetime in each case. Because Deepgram's `created` field is documented UTC, an offset-less input is interpreted as UTC (not naive and not localized to any other zone). This proves it is not a bare fromisoformat, which on Python 3.9 rejects a trailing 'Z' and returns a naive datetime for a no-offset string.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test asserts ALL THREE with exact pinned values: (a) parsing '2024-01-15T08:30:00.123456Z' yields datetime(2024,1,15,8,30,0,123456,tzinfo=timezone.utc); (b) parsing '2024-01-15T08:30:00' yields datetime(2024,1,15,8,30,0,tzinfo=timezone.utc) (offset-less interpreted as UTC); (c) parsing '2024-01-15T08:30:00.5' yields datetime(2024,1,15,8,30,0,500000,tzinfo=timezone.utc).

criteria.description: A UTC→local conversion function completes the bidirectional (↔) frame: given a timezone-aware UTC datetime it returns the equivalent America/Los_Angeles wall-clock time, correct across DST — a summer (PDT) instant maps back at UTC−7 and a winter (PST) instant at UTC−8.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test asserts with pinned values: (summer) UTC datetime(2024,7,15,19,0,0,tzinfo=timezone.utc) → local wall-clock 2024-07-15 12:00:00 (hour==12, PDT); (winter) UTC datetime(2024,1,15,20,0,0,tzinfo=timezone.utc) → local wall-clock 2024-01-15 12:00:00 (hour==12, PST). Assertions compare the resulting America/Los_Angeles wall-clock components (year/month/day/hour/minute).

criteria.description: Local→UTC conversion treats a naive input as America/Los_Angeles local time and returns a timezone-aware UTC datetime, correct across DST: a Pacific Daylight Time timestamp converts by adding 7 hours (UTC−7) and a Pacific Standard Time timestamp by adding 8 hours (UTC−8).
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test asserts a summer date (naive local datetime(2024,7,15,12,0,0) → datetime(2024,7,15,19,0,0,tzinfo=timezone.utc), +7h) and a winter date (naive local datetime(2024,1,15,12,0,0) → datetime(2024,1,15,20,0,0,tzinfo=timezone.utc), +8h), each returned as a timezone-aware UTC datetime with the exact pinned instant.

criteria.description: test_deepgram_request_audit.py exists in the repo root and is discoverable and passing under `python3 -m unittest discover -v`, fully offline with stdlib only (no network, no secrets file, no third-party imports).
criteria.verifyBy: Run `python3 -m unittest discover -v`; all tests pass with no network access and no ~/.voice-tutor-secrets.env present.

scope.description: Only create deepgram_request_audit.py and test_deepgram_request_audit.py in the repo root; do not add unrelated modules, config, or packaging.

stdlib only — no requests, pytz, or other third-party packages; timezones MUST use stdlib zoneinfo.ZoneInfo('America/Los_Angeles') (not pytz and not a hardcoded fixed offset), HTTP (later sprints) via urllib.request.
Runtime must be read-only; parsing/conversion functions in this sprint must not write, create, or modify any file.
Never print, log, or echo credential values anywhere.
